name: InverseFinance
report: The attacker exploited the protocol's reliance on a flawed oracle implementation that uses pool asset balances to compute LP token prices. By manipulating the underlying Curve pool's composition via a large WBTC-to-USDT swap, the attacker inflated the reported value of yvcrv3Crypto collateral. This allowed them to borrow far more DOLA than justified. The oracle likely used a function like latestAnswer (or equivalent) that returns a price derived directly from on-chain balances without time-weighted averaging or manipulation resistance, enabling instantaneous price distortion.
